channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Why is it so hard for the U.S. to win wars?
item.description: U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. presidents have promised short, decisive wars in Afghanistan, Iraq and Iran. All have proved much more difficult than advertised and fallen far short of the political goals set at the beginning.

(Image credit: Brennan Linsley)
item.dc:creator: Greg Myre

item.title: Opinion: The continued courage of Captain Sully
item.description: Retired pilot Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger is known for safely landing an airplane on the Hudson River in 2009. This week, he announced that he has Alzheimer's disease.
item.content:encoded:
Retired pilot Chesley "Sully" Sullenberger is known for safely landing an airplane on the Hudson River in 2009. This week, he announced that he has Alzheimer's disease.

(Image credit: Chip Somodevilla)
item.dc:creator: Scott Simon

item.title: 8 killed and more than 60 wounded in Ukrainian drone attack on Russian regions
item.description: Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.
item.content:encoded:
Kyiv's forces are continuing their aerial campaign against energy infrastructure and military targets inside Russia, aiming to undermine Moscow's war effort.

(Image credit: Moscow Region Governor Andrei Vo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The 2000s called. They want their digital camera back
item.description: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.content:encoded: Why are people who weren't born 25 years ago snapping up the digital camera of that era? Blame Taylor Swift, trend cycles, childhood nostalgia and smartphone fatigue.
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: D'oh! I can't believe I did that! Graceful ways to handle awkward moments
item.description: Yes, there is a way to gracefully address that you've been walking around with spinach in your teeth. Here are tactics to quickly defuse your most embarrassing, cringe-inducing moments.
item.content:encoded: Yes, there is a way to gracefully address that you've been walking around with spinach in your teeth. Here are tactics to quickly defuse your most embarrassing, cringe-inducing moments.
item.dc:creator: Malaka Gharib

item.title: The U.S. and Iran blow past red lines as they lurch back toward all-out war
item.description: The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.
item.content:encoded:
The United States and Iran exchanged strikes aimed at infrastructure and military targets on Saturday as their battle over the Strait of Hormuz intensified.

(Image credit: Razieh Poudat/AP Photo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: ICE shared Medicaid data it wasn't supposed to have with Palantir
item.description: The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.
item.content:encoded:
The revelations came out in a federal court case brought by Democratic states challenging ICE's access to Medicaid data to aid in deportation efforts.

(Image credit: Adam Gray)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Ex-wife says ICE agent who killed man in Maine had racist beliefs, violent tendencies
item.description: Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.
item.content:encoded:
Ashley Brouillette has identified her ex-husband, David Brouillette, as the officer who fatally shot Joan Sebastián Durán Guerrero in Biddeford, Maine, on Monday. She said she learned he was the officer responsible when he called her on Wednesday.

(Image credit: Yuki Iwamura)
item.dc:creator: Vanessa Romo

item.title: Spain could make World Cup history: The first to win men's and women's trophies back-to-back
item.description: The Spanish Men's National Team will face Argentina in Sunday's World Cup final. The country's women's team lifted its first World Cup trophy in 2023.
item.content:encoded:
The Spanish Men's National Team will face Argentina in Sunday's World Cup final. The country's women's team lifted its first World Cup trophy in 2023.

(Image credit: Florencia Tan Jun)
item.dc:creator: Madeline Fox

item.title: Move over, Super Bowl? There's an even bigger – and splashier – World Cup halftime show coming
item.description: The show, which will begin somewhere around 3:45 PM ET, will air in the U.S. on Fox, Fox One and in Spanish on Telemundo, as well as on the Fox Sports app and streaming in Spanish on Peacock. It's expected to run for about 11 minutes.
item.content:encoded:
The show, which will begin somewhere around 3:45 PM ET, will air in the U.S. on Fox, Fox One and in Spanish on Telemundo, as well as on the Fox Sports app and streaming in Spanish on Peacock. It's expected to run for about 11 minutes.

(Image credit: Charly Triballeau)
item.dc:creator: Anastasia Tsioulcas
